note: Compact merged life-impact/support catalog. Repeated PDF excerpts and legacy wrappers were removed; support limits/accommodations/pacing content retained.

sources.medical_safety_note: This JSON is an accessibility/life-impact catalogue derived from the attached PDF and cautious general health-literacy reasoning. It is not a diagnostic tool, treatment plan, or medical advice. Individual impact varies, especially with comorbid conditions, environment, support, money, housing, work demands, flare cycles, and care access.

entries.name: Addison's Disease
life_limitations: May limit stamina, pain tolerance, immune safety, digestion, breathing, sleep, temperature tolerance, medical scheduling, or physical reliability.
Can make work/school, errands, self-care, food planning, socializing, and travel harder due to symptoms, flares, treatment, or environmental triggers.
Invisible symptoms may be misunderstood, causing pressure to overexert.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Medical recognition and accommodations can support safer pacing, trigger reduction, treatment access, and clearer expectations.
Adaptive routines can slightly improve autonomy and reduce flares or crashes.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost often fluctuates with flares, treatment side effects, pain, fatigue, weather, food, sleep, and stress.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Aging
life_limitations: May limit stamina, pain tolerance, immune safety, digestion, breathing, sleep, temperature tolerance, medical scheduling, or physical reliability.
Can make work/school, errands, self-care, food planning, socializing, and travel harder due to symptoms, flares, treatment, or environmental triggers.

entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost often fluctuates with flares, treatment side effects, pain, fatigue, weather, food, sleep, and stress.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Albinism
life_limitations: May limit access to visual, auditory, or color-coded information, navigation, communication, safety cues, and digital/print materials.
Can make meetings, alarms, crowded spaces, signage, reading, driving, or screen work harder without accessible formats.
Barriers often come from inaccessible communication rather than the sensory difference alone.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Accessible formats, captions, interpreters, tactile/visual alerts, high contrast, and assistive technology can greatly improve access.
Designing information in more than one sensory mode can slightly improve independence and reduce missed information.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost rises when the person must constantly compensate for missing or inaccessible information.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Alcoholism
life_limitations: May limit concentration, emotional regulation, reliability, sleep, stress tolerance, communication, social safety, or ability to handle conflict and change.
Can make work/school, appointments, self-care, relationships, and public settings harder even if the disability is invisible.
Stigma and fear of disclosure may create extra barriers to accommodations.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Disclosure to safe people or formal accommodation processes can open access to flexible schedules, quiet space, written instructions, and supportive check-ins.
Skills, treatment, routines, and validation can slightly improve consistency and reduce shame.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Mental load may drain spoons before visible tasks start, especially under stress or masking.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Allergies
life_limitations: May limit stamina, pain tolerance, immune safety, digestion, breathing, sleep, temperature tolerance, medical scheduling, or physical reliability.

Invisible symptoms may be misunderstood, causing pressure to overexert.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Medical recognition and accommodations can support safer pacing, trigger reduction, treatment access, and clearer expectations.
Adaptive routines can slightly improve autonomy and reduce flares or crashes.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost often fluctuates with flares, treatment side effects, pain, fatigue, weather, food, sleep, and stress.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Alzheimer's Disease
life_limitations: May limit memory, coordination, seizure safety, movement, balance, speech, stamina, pain control, cognition, or independence.
Can make driving, machinery, stairs, deadlines, public settings, and complex tasks unsafe or inconsistent during flares/episodes.
Symptoms may be episodic or progressive, requiring flexible planning.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Safety plans, assistive technology, therapy, routines, and flexible expectations can improve predictability and reduce risk.
Recognizing triggers/episodes can slightly improve planning and confidence.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost varies sharply with episodes, fatigue, medication effects, and sensory/cognitive load.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Amputation
life_limitations: May limit walking, standing, lifting, reaching, transfers, balance, speed, endurance, pain control, and access to inaccessible spaces.
Can make errands, bathing, cooking, transportation, cleaning, work sites, and social activities dependent on ramps, seating, rest, or assistance.
Fall risk, pain flares, and inaccessible buildings can reduce independence even when the person has strong coping skills.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Mobility aids, ergonomic tools, accessible layouts, and pacing can improve safety, range, and dignity.
Planning around energy, pain, and access can slightly increase participation and reduce injury.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Physical spoon cost rises with distance, stairs, carrying, transfers, and repeated movements.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Intellectual Disability
life_limitations: May limit communication, executive functioning, sensory tolerance, reading/writing, attention, social navigation, or learning in standard environments.
Can make multitasking, vague instructions, loud spaces, transitions, or rapid deadlines disproportionately exhausting.
The person may be capable but needs a different format, pace, or sensory setup.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Accommodations can reveal strengths by removing unnecessary barriers in communication, timing, sensory load, or format.
Assistive technology, visual supports, and predictable routines can slightly improve independence and task completion.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost increases with sensory overload, transitions, masking, and executive-function demands.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Attention Deficit/Hyperactivity
life_limitations: May limit communication, executive functioning, sensory tolerance, reading/writing, attention, social navigation, or learning in standard environments.
Can make multitasking, vague instructions, loud spaces, transitions, or rapid deadlines disproportionately exhausting.
The person may be capable but needs a different format, pace, or sensory setup.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Accommodations can reveal strengths by removing unnecessary barriers in communication, timing, sensory load, or format.
Assistive technology, visual supports, and predictable routines can slightly improve independence and task completion.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost increases with sensory overload, transitions, masking, and executive-function demands.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Bipolar Disorder
life_limitations: May limit concentration, emotional regulation, reliability, sleep, stress tolerance, communication, social safety, or ability to handle conflict and change.
Can make work/school, appointments, self-care, relationships, and public settings harder even if the disability is invisible.

possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Disclosure to safe people or formal accommodation processes can open access to flexible schedules, quiet space, written instructions, and supportive check-ins.
Skills, treatment, routines, and validation can slightly improve consistency and reduce shame.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Mental load may drain spoons before visible tasks start, especially under stress or masking.

entries.name: Blindness
life_limitations: May limit access to visual, auditory, or color-coded information, navigation, communication, safety cues, and digital/print materials.
Can make meetings, alarms, crowded spaces, signage, reading, driving, or screen work harder without accessible formats.
Barriers often come from inaccessible communication rather than the sensory difference alone.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Accessible formats, captions, interpreters, tactile/visual alerts, high contrast, and assistive technology can greatly improve access.
Designing information in more than one sensory mode can slightly improve independence and reduce missed information.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost rises when the person must constantly compensate for missing or inaccessible information.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Brain Injury
life_limitations: May limit memory, coordination, seizure safety, movement, balance, speech, stamina, pain control, cognition, or independence.
Can make driving, machinery, stairs, deadlines, public settings, and complex tasks unsafe or inconsistent during flares/episodes.
Symptoms may be episodic or progressive, requiring flexible planning.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Safety plans, assistive technology, therapy, routines, and flexible expectations can improve predictability and reduce risk.
Recognizing triggers/episodes can slightly improve planning and confidence.

entries.name: Absence seizure
life_limitations: Can affect movement, balance, speech, coordination, awareness, stamina, pain, safety, driving, work/school, and public confidence.
Symptoms can be unpredictable or progressive, making planning harder and increasing fall/injury risk.
Visible symptoms may attract stigma even when the person is competent and aware.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Recognition can support medication review, mobility/safety planning, assistive devices, therapy, and emergency plans.
Matching supports to the symptom pattern can slightly improve independence and reduce injury risk.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Physical and cognitive spoon cost may fluctuate; safety-sensitive tasks may require backup.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Abulia
entries.source_group: mental health / psychiatric / neurological condition listed by source
life_limitations: May limit daily functioning through symptoms that affect mood, thinking, behavior, relationships, body comfort, sleep, communication, or safety.
Can create extra effort for tasks that other people may consider automatic, especially under stress or without accommodations.
Stigma and misunderstanding can add social and practical barriers beyond the symptoms themselves.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: A clear name can help the person seek information, support, accommodations, and more compassionate self-understanding.

life_limitations: Can disrupt nourishment, body trust, social eating, medical stability, concentration, energy, mood, and daily routines around food.
May create fear, shame, secrecy, rigidity, gastrointestinal strain, or medical risk when eating, not eating, purging, or restrictive rules become central.
Meals can become high-energy tasks instead of automatic daily care.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Recognition can open access to structured meal support, medical monitoring, therapy, and less shame-based communication.
Gentle routines and safe-food planning can slightly improve stability and reduce decision fatigue.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Food tasks can carry high physical and emotional spoon costs; support should reduce decisions and shame.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Caffeine Addiction
life_limitations: Can reduce control over time, money, attention, sleep, nutrition, relationships, and daily responsibilities through cravings, tolerance, withdrawal, or compulsive use.
May increase health risks, conflict, financial strain, legal/work/school problems, secrecy, shame, and unsafe situations.
Recovery work itself can take substantial time and energy for appointments, coping skills, medication, peer support, and avoiding triggers.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Naming the pattern can make it easier to seek harm-reduction, treatment, peer support, and boundaries rather than treating it as a moral failure.
Tracking triggers and use patterns can slightly improve safety, planning, relapse prevention, and honest communication with trusted supports.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: High and variable spoon cost; withdrawal, cravings, recovery work, and trigger avoidance can consume energy before normal tasks begin.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Catatonic disorder
life_limitations: Can affect perception, trust, reality-testing, communication, sleep, safety, decision-making, relationships, and ability to work or study consistently.
Hallucinations, delusions, disorganization, or catatonic/motor symptoms can make ordinary environments frightening, confusing, or unsafe.
Stigma may make the person less likely to ask for help and may worsen isolation.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Early recognition can support treatment, crisis planning, trusted reality-checking, medication review, and safer routines.
A nonjudgmental support network can slightly improve safety, reduce fear, and help the person stay connected.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: High spoon cost during active symptoms; safety, sleep, and low-stimulation routines may need priority over productivity.

entries.name: Childhood amnesia
life_limitations: Can affect memory, orientation, learning new information, decision-making, safety, routines, finances, medication management, travel, and independence.
May require reminders, supervision, simplified environments, or caregiver support to prevent missed tasks or unsafe situations.
Confusion or memory gaps can be frightening and socially isolating.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Recognition can allow memory aids, routines, legal/care planning, environmental labels, and support before crises happen.

entries.spoon_energy_notes: Cognitive spoon cost increases when tasks require recall, navigation, new learning, or multitasking.

entries.name: Circadian Rhythm Disorders
life_limitations: Can reduce daytime energy, focus, mood stability, pain tolerance, reaction time, immune resilience, and ability to keep schedules.
May limit driving, work/school consistency, social life, self-care, and safety when sleep is disrupted or attacks occur.
Poor sleep can worsen other physical and mental health conditions.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Naming the sleep pattern can support sleep hygiene, medical evaluation, schedule accommodations, and safety planning.
Predictable rest windows and lower morning demands can slightly improve daily reliability.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Often reduces starting spoon count for the whole day; sleep loss makes every later task cost more.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Claustrophobia
life_limitations: Can shrink the person’s world through avoidance, panic, hypervigilance, intrusive worry, and fear of places/situations where escape or safety feels uncertain.
May limit appointments, errands, work/school, social connection, sleep, concentration, travel, conflict resolution, and asking for help.
The person may appear “fine” while spending a large amount of internal energy monitoring threat or preventing a panic/trauma response.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Recognizing the condition can support grounding skills, exposure at a tolerable pace, trauma-informed care, and clearer boundaries.
Predictable routines, safety plans, and permission to pause can slightly improve autonomy and reduce shame.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Variable spoon cost; even short tasks may become expensive when they involve crowds, uncertainty, conflict, trauma cues, or sensory overload.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

May make school/work, forms, appointments, chores, hygiene, crowded places, multitasking, or verbal instructions harder without accommodations.
Mismatch between the person’s needs and the environment can be more disabling than the diagnosis alone.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Understanding the profile can unlock accommodations, assistive technology, communication supports, routines, special interests, and strengths-based planning.
Clear structure and sensory-respectful environments can slightly improve independence, confidence, and task completion.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost often rises with transitions, sensory overload, unclear instructions, masking, or executive-function demands.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Intermittent Explosive Disorder
life_limitations: Can create urges or repetitive behaviors that interfere with safety, skin/hair/body integrity, money, time, legal risk, relationships, or self-esteem.
May be worsened by stress, boredom, shame, sensory discomfort, or emotional overload.
The person may need support without punishment because shame can make cycles harder to interrupt.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Recognition can support habit-reversal strategies, safer substitutes, environmental changes, and earlier help-seeking.
Tracking urges can slightly improve control and reduce harm over time.
support_or_accommodation_ideas: reduced access to triggers when safe
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost rises with urges and after-effects; prevention and safer substitutions can conserve energy.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Major Depression
life_limitations: Can affect energy, motivation, sleep, appetite, self-care, emotional regulation, follow-through, pleasure, and hope.
May make chores, hygiene, work/school, communication, socializing, and long-term planning feel heavy or inconsistent.
Mood swings or episodes can disrupt routines and relationships even when the person is trying hard.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: A clear label can help the person track cycles, ask for support earlier, and use treatment/routine strategies before symptoms snowball.
Mood tracking and compassionate pacing can slightly improve predictability, self-advocacy, and recovery time after difficult days.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon availability may change by episode/day; tiny tasks may need to be split into steps with rest and reinforcement.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Major depressive disorder
entries.source_group: mental health / psychiatric / neurological condition listed by source
life_limitations: May limit daily functioning through symptoms that affect mood, thinking, behavior, relationships, body comfort, sleep, communication, or safety.
Can create extra effort for tasks that other people may consider automatic, especially under stress or without accommodations.
Stigma and misunderstanding can add social and practical barriers beyond the symptoms themselves.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: A clear name can help the person seek information, support, accommodations, and more compassionate self-understanding.
Tracking patterns can slightly improve planning, communication, and access to help.
support_or_accommodation_ideas: professional care if distress or impairment is significant
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost depends on symptom severity, support, sleep, stress, comorbidities, and environment.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Narcolepsy
life_limitations: Can reduce daytime energy, focus, mood stability, pain tolerance, reaction time, immune resilience, and ability to keep schedules.
May limit driving, work/school consistency, social life, self-care, and safety when sleep is disrupted or attacks occur.
Poor sleep can worsen other physical and mental health conditions.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Naming the sleep pattern can support sleep hygiene, medical evaluation, schedule accommodations, and safety planning.
Predictable rest windows and lower morning demands can slightly improve daily reliability.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Often reduces starting spoon count for the whole day; sleep loss makes every later task cost more.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Neglect of child
life_limitations: Can involve unsafe relationships, neglect, abuse, or relational distress that affects housing, trust, emotional regulation, safety, sleep, and daily stability.
May reduce autonomy if the person depends on unsafe people or lacks support.
Trauma and practical consequences can interfere with work/school, care, and self-protection.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Naming the issue can support safety planning, advocacy, protective services, therapy, and healthier boundaries.
Stable support and practical resources can slightly improve safety and decision-making.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: High spoon cost when safety is uncertain; stabilization may need to come before productivity.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Opioid Addiction
life_limitations: Can reduce control over time, money, attention, sleep, nutrition, relationships, and daily responsibilities through cravings, tolerance, withdrawal, or compulsive use.
May increase health risks, conflict, financial strain, legal/work/school problems, secrecy, shame, and unsafe situations.
Recovery work itself can take substantial time and energy for appointments, coping skills, medication, peer support, and avoiding triggers.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Naming the pattern can make it easier to seek harm-reduction, treatment, peer support, and boundaries rather than treating it as a moral failure.
Tracking triggers and use patterns can slightly improve safety, planning, relapse prevention, and honest communication with trusted supports.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: High and variable spoon cost; withdrawal, cravings, recovery work, and trigger avoidance can consume energy before normal tasks begin.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Orthorexia (ON)
life_limitations: Can disrupt nourishment, body trust, social eating, medical stability, concentration, energy, mood, and daily routines around food.
May create fear, shame, secrecy, rigidity, gastrointestinal strain, or medical risk when eating, not eating, purging, or restrictive rules become central.
Meals can become high-energy tasks instead of automatic daily care.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Recognition can open access to structured meal support, medical monitoring, therapy, and less shame-based communication.
Gentle routines and safe-food planning can slightly improve stability and reduce decision fatigue.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Food tasks can carry high physical and emotional spoon costs; support should reduce decisions and shame.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Pain disorder
life_limitations: Can make body symptoms, pain, health fears, or medical interactions consume large amounts of energy and attention.
May limit activity, work/school, social trust, and confidence in one’s body, especially when symptoms are misunderstood.
Repeated appointments, uncertainty, and stigma can add burden.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Recognition can support coordinated care, symptom tracking, pacing, and communication that validates distress without escalating fear.
A clear care plan can slightly reduce uncertainty and repeated crisis cycles.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost increases with symptom flares, uncertainty, appointments, and pain.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.

entries.name: Panic attacks
life_limitations: Can shrink the person’s world through avoidance, panic, hypervigilance, intrusive worry, and fear of places/situations where escape or safety feels uncertain.
May limit appointments, errands, work/school, social connection, sleep, concentration, travel, conflict resolution, and asking for help.
The person may appear “fine” while spending a large amount of internal energy monitoring threat or preventing a panic/trauma response.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Recognizing the condition can support grounding skills, exposure at a tolerable pace, trauma-informed care, and clearer boundaries.

entries.name: Adrenal Insufficiency
life_limitations: Hormone imbalance can affect multiple body systems, so symptoms may interfere with energy, mood, body temperature, appetite/weight, sleep, concentration, strength, blood pressure, and daily reliability.
Can limit stamina, stress tolerance, hydration/salt balance, illness recovery, and safety during physical stress because cortisol regulation is impaired.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Diagnosis and monitoring can slightly improve life by making symptoms explainable, treatable, and easier to plan around.
A steroid/emergency plan and predictable medication access can improve safety and confidence.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost depends on whether hormones are stable, undertreated, overtreated, or affected by illness/stress.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
entries.source_condition_description: adrenal glands do not produce enough steroid hormones, especially cortisol, affecting sodium, potassium, and water balance

entries.name: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH)
life_limitations: Hormone imbalance can affect multiple body systems, so symptoms may interfere with energy, mood, body temperature, appetite/weight, sleep, concentration, strength, blood pressure, and daily reliability.
Can affect metabolism, immune function, blood pressure, growth/puberty/sex hormones, and illness stress depending on type and severity.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Diagnosis and monitoring can slightly improve life by making symptoms explainable, treatable, and easier to plan around.
Early recognition and hormone management can improve stability, development, and emergency preparedness.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost depends on whether hormones are stable, undertreated, overtreated, or affected by illness/stress.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
entries.source_condition_description: inherited adrenal enzyme disorders affecting hormone production for metabolism, immune function, blood pressure, and essential body functions

entries.name: Hyperaldosteronism
life_limitations: Hormone imbalance can affect multiple body systems, so symptoms may interfere with energy, mood, body temperature, appetite/weight, sleep, concentration, strength, blood pressure, and daily reliability.
High blood pressure and low potassium can contribute to headaches, weakness, cramps, fatigue, heart strain, and reduced exercise tolerance.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Diagnosis and monitoring can slightly improve life by making symptoms explainable, treatable, and easier to plan around.
Blood pressure/potassium monitoring and treatment can improve safety and reduce cardiovascular strain.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost depends on whether hormones are stable, undertreated, overtreated, or affected by illness/stress.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
entries.source_condition_description: too much aldosterone causing high blood pressure and low potassium

entries.name: Osteoporosis
life_limitations: Hormone imbalance can affect multiple body systems, so symptoms may interfere with energy, mood, body temperature, appetite/weight, sleep, concentration, strength, blood pressure, and daily reliability.
Fragile bones can limit lifting, bending, falls risk, high-impact activities, and confidence moving around; fractures can severely disrupt independence.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Diagnosis and monitoring can slightly improve life by making symptoms explainable, treatable, and easier to plan around.
Fall prevention, strength/balance work, bone-health treatment, and home safety changes can improve mobility confidence.

entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
entries.source_condition_description: bone tissue deterioration and reduced bone strength causing fragility and fracture risk

entries.name: Pituitary Disorders
life_limitations: Hormone imbalance can affect multiple body systems, so symptoms may interfere with energy, mood, body temperature, appetite/weight, sleep, concentration, strength, blood pressure, and daily reliability.
Can affect many hormone-controlled functions such as growth, fertility, lactation, thyroid/adrenal function, energy, headaches, vision, and mood depending on the hormone involved.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Diagnosis and monitoring can slightly improve life by making symptoms explainable, treatable, and easier to plan around.
Specialist monitoring can connect seemingly unrelated symptoms and guide hormone replacement or other treatment.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost depends on whether hormones are stable, undertreated, overtreated, or affected by illness/stress.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
entries.source_condition_description: pituitary growths or disorders that can create too much or too little hormones regulating important body functions

entries.name: Thyroid Disorders
life_limitations: Hormone imbalance can affect multiple body systems, so symptoms may interfere with energy, mood, body temperature, appetite/weight, sleep, concentration, strength, blood pressure, and daily reliability.
Can affect metabolism, energy, temperature, protein synthesis, heart rhythm, weight, anxiety/calm, and sleep.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Diagnosis and monitoring can slightly improve life by making symptoms explainable, treatable, and easier to plan around.
Testing and treatment can make body-process changes easier to understand and manage.

entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
entries.source_condition_description: thyroid hormone issues affecting metabolism, protein synthesis, and possibly thyroid cancer or other disease

entries.name: Hypothyroidism
life_limitations: Hormone imbalance can affect multiple body systems, so symptoms may interfere with energy, mood, body temperature, appetite/weight, sleep, concentration, strength, blood pressure, and daily reliability.
May limit energy, warmth, mood, focus, digestion, weight regulation, heart rate, skin/hair comfort, and motivation through slowed body processes.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Diagnosis and monitoring can slightly improve life by making symptoms explainable, treatable, and easier to plan around.
Correct thyroid replacement and routine tracking can restore more predictable energy for many people.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Spoon cost depends on whether hormones are stable, undertreated, overtreated, or affected by illness/stress.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
entries.source_condition_description: underactive thyroid with too little thyroid hormone

entries.name: Hyperthyroidism
life_limitations: Hormone imbalance can affect multiple body systems, so symptoms may interfere with energy, mood, body temperature, appetite/weight, sleep, concentration, strength, blood pressure, and daily reliability.
May limit sleep, calm, heart-rate safety, heat tolerance, weight stability, hand steadiness, and concentration through sped-up body processes.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Diagnosis and monitoring can slightly improve life by making symptoms explainable, treatable, and easier to plan around.
Treatment and trigger/symptom tracking can improve sleep, safety, and anxiety-like physical symptoms.

entries.source_condition_description: overactive thyroid with too much thyroid hormone

entries.name: Spoon Theory / limited daily energy
life_limitations: Can limit how many tasks, appointments, chores, social plans, and care activities fit into a day.
Forces tradeoffs: doing one task may mean losing capacity for another, even when the person wants to do both.
Can make invisible effort hard for others to understand.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Provides a simple shared language for boundaries, pacing, task prioritization, and compassion.
Can slightly improve planning by making energy visible and countable.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Every task should have an estimated spoon cost and a recovery option.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
entries.concept_description: A person has a limited amount of physical and mental energy to spend on daily tasks.

entries.name: Day-to-day variability
life_limitations: Makes future planning difficult because tomorrow’s capacity may not match today’s.
Can cause guilt or conflict when commitments must change.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Encourages flexible planning, backup options, and permission to reschedule without shame.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Use ranges rather than fixed costs; include flare/crash mode.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
entries.concept_description: Spoon cost for the same task can vary by day.

entries.name: Invisible illness visibility
life_limitations: Can cause disbelief, pressure to overperform, lack of accommodations, and social misunderstanding.
May make people feel obligated to explain private health details.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: A shared term such as “spoonie” can help people find community and explain needs quickly if they choose.
support_or_accommodation_ideas: avoid judging how energy is spent
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Social invalidation itself costs spoons.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
entries.concept_description: Many chronic conditions are not immediately visible.

entries.name: Community and peer support
life_limitations: Without community, chronic illness/disability can feel isolating and misunderstood.
Peer spaces can still be tiring if comparison or unsolicited advice becomes overwhelming.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Peer support can offer emotional support, practical tips, identity validation, and reduced isolation.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Community can return spoons emotionally, but only when it is safe and not demanding.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
entries.concept_description: The spoonie community can offer shared experience and support.

entries.name: Boundary communication
life_limitations: People may still judge how someone spends limited energy or expect justification.
The person may need to protect energy by saying no.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Can make it easier to ask for help, explain cancellations, and honor needs without over-explaining.
entries.spoon_energy_notes: Boundary-setting may cost spoons at first but can prevent larger crashes later.
entries.individual_variability_note: Impact can be mild, moderate, severe, intermittent, progressive, or invisible depending on the person and context.
entries.concept_description: Spoon language can communicate limits without a full medical explanation.
